text:
2025/0390(APP) | Coal and Steel Fund Expiry
Rapporteur: Johan Van Overtveldt (ECR) | Lead committee: BUDG

📅 17-07-2026 | 📜 Published in the Official Journal
What's next: It's law. It enters into force on the date it specifies or, if none, the 20th day after publication in the Official Journal.

Summary: This legislation addresses the financial arrangements that result from the end of a treaty relating to coal and steel, and implements provisions for managing a research fund connected to these industries.
[AI-Generated]

Source: OEIL procedure file 2025/0390(APP)
title: Financial consequences of the expiry of the ECSC Treaty and Research Fund for Coal and Steel: implementation of Protocol No 37
summary: This legislation addresses the financial arrangements that result from the end of a treaty relating to coal and steel, and implements provisions for managing a research fund connected to these industries.

text:
2025/0417(COD) | Trade Rules and Product Labelling Amendment
Rapporteur: Camilla Laureti (S&D) | Lead committee: AGRI

📅 14-07-2026 | 🧭 Negotiating mandate for trilogue talks
What's next: Trilogue talks with the Council can begin once the Council side has its own mandate. Closed-door rounds follow; timing is not published.

Summary: This proposal aims to update the rules for how certain products are made, labeled, and certified, as well as how they can be bought and sold with countries outside the EU. The changes would modernize these existing regulations to reflect current needs.
[AI-Generated]

Source: OEIL procedure file 2025/0417(COD)
title: Amending certain production, labelling and certification rules and certain rules on trade with third countries
summary: This proposal aims to update the rules for how certain products are made, labeled, and certified, as well as how they can be bought and sold with countries outside the EU. The changes would modernize these existing regulations to reflect current needs.

text:
2025/0407(COD) | Austria-Switzerland Cross-Border Bus Cabotage
Rapporteur: Elissavet Vozemberg-Vrionidi (EPP) | Lead committee: TRAN

📅 17-07-2026 | 📜 Published in the Official Journal
What's next: It's law. It enters into force on the date it specifies or, if none, the 20th day after publication in the Official Journal.

Summary: Austria is given permission to update its road transport agreement with Switzerland to allow coach and bus operators to pick up and drop off passengers within each other's border regions while providing international services. This change facilitates easier cross-border passenger transport by coach and bus between the two countries.
[AI-Generated]

Source: OEIL procedure file 2025/0407(COD)
title: Empowering Austria to amend its existing bilateral road transport agreement with Switzerland with a view to authorising cabotage operations in the course of the provision of international road passenger transport services by coach and bus in the border regions between the two countries
summary: Austria is given permission to update its road transport agreement with Switzerland to allow coach and bus operators to pick up and drop off passengers within each other's border regions while providing international services. This change facilitates easier cross-border passenger transport by coach and bus between the two countries.

text:
2026/0203(COD) | Electricity Bills Future-Proofing Regulation | Regulation
European Commission

📅 17-07-2026 | 📋 New legislative proposal
What's next: Parliament's lead committee and the Council's working party examine it in parallel. No deadline applies at this stage; most files take one to two-plus years.

Summary: This proposal seeks to modify rules on electricity pricing to make bills more affordable and sustainable by lowering overall system costs and encouraging more people and businesses to switch to electric power and digital technologies.
[AI-Generated]

Source: OEIL procedure file 2026/0203(COD)
title: Proposal for a REGULATION OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL amending Regulation (EU) 2019/943, as regards future-proofing electricity bills in the Union, through reducing system costs and fostering electrification and digitalisation
summary: This proposal seeks to modify rules on electricity pricing to make bills more affordable and sustainable by lowering overall system costs and encouraging more people and businesses to switch to electric power and digital technologies.

text:
2026/0211(COD) | EU Emissions Trading Benchmarks Revision | Regulation
European Commission

📅 17-07-2026 | 📋 New legislative proposal
What's next: Parliament's lead committee and the Council's working party examine it in parallel. No deadline applies at this stage; most files take one to two-plus years.

Summary: This proposal aims to update the standard reference values used to measure heat and fuel efficiency under the EU's emissions trading system for the years 2026 to 2030. The revised benchmarks would establish new targets for how much heat and fuel different industries should use compared to the best performers in their sector.
[AI-Generated]

Source: OEIL procedure file 2026/0211(COD)
title: Proposal for a REGULATION OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL amending Directive 2003/87/EC as regards revised benchmark values for the heat and fuel benchmarks for the period from 2026 to 2030
summary: This proposal aims to update the standard reference values used to measure heat and fuel efficiency under the EU's emissions trading system for the years 2026 to 2030. The revised benchmarks would establish new targets for how much heat and fuel different industries should use compared to the best performers in their sector.

text:
2026/0210(COD) | Shipping Emissions Reporting Streamlined | Regulation
European Commission

📅 17-07-2026 | 📋 New legislative proposal
What's next: Parliament's lead committee and the Council's working party examine it in parallel. No deadline applies at this stage; most files take one to two-plus years.

Summary: This proposal would simplify how shipping companies monitor and report their greenhouse gas emissions. It aims to align these reporting requirements with changes being made to the broader system for tracking and trading emissions across Europe.
[AI-Generated]

Source: OEIL procedure file 2026/0210(COD)
title: Proposal for a REGULATION OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL amending Regulations (EU) 2015/757 and (EU) 2023/1805 to simplify and streamline monitoring, reporting and verification and to align with revisions of the EU Emissions Trading System
summary: This proposal would simplify how shipping companies monitor and report their greenhouse gas emissions. It aims to align these reporting requirements with changes being made to the broader system for tracking and trading emissions across Europe.
